data.body: [OPERATIONAL AC #2 SATISFIED] Capped live heal with bilateral pre-heal backup — DEMONSTRATED end-to-end against live Jira (REB). Evidence: (1) Synthetic bound pair created: local ticket 8519-a58a-8af4-4830 (tagged test-artifact,a118-heal-probe) <-> REB-877 (bridge-created, status 'To Do'). (2) BILATERAL PRE-HEAL BACKUP (abort-if-either-fails, verified BOTH OK before proceeding): Jira-side = REST export of REB-877 full JSON -> pre-heal-REB-877-20260710T005736Z.json (5476 bytes, captured status 'To Do'); tickets-branch = git tag pre-heal-a118-20260710T005736Z @ 2a77dd482, pushed to origin. (3) Archived 8519 (open->archived) -> dropped from 'rebar list' (off-snapshot) while still readable via 'rebar show' (archived=true) -> binding_walk drift-class-A target. (4) CAPPED HEAL APPLIED by reconcile-bridge run 29061224115: binding_walk classified TERMINAL -> TERMINAL_TRANSITION, applied 'update REB-877 {status: Done}' (log: RECON: batch_outcome action=update key=REB-877 error=None). REST-verified REB-877 status To Do (pre) -> Done (post). BREAKER ALLOWED: the pass applied the update (a tripped breaker REFUSES the entire acting phase); 1 acting decision / ~854 bindings = 0.0012 << max_acting_fraction 0.10 = capped. Non-destructive (terminal-transition, not deletion). Proceeding to guaranteed bilateral cleanup (delete REB-877 + purge 8519).
